description: Add MeetingRoom React component suite in packages/shared-ui for WebRTC frontend integration using livekit-client. Includes camera/mic permission flow, device picker, network quality indicator, simulcast, screen share, raise-hand, reactions, chat sidecar, speaker/grid/spotlight views, per-participant volume slider, push-to-talk, background blur, knock flow for locked rooms, auto-mute on silence, polls, and breakout room UI.
steps: Add livekit-client as a dependency to packages/shared-ui/package.json

Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/MeetingRoom.tsx - top-level component that connects to a LiveKit room via access token. Manages room state, publishes/subscribes tracks. Props: token, serverUrl, roomName, onDisconnect.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/DevicePicker.tsx - lists available cameras/mics via navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices(), allows selection, handles permission prompts.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/ParticipantTile.tsx - renders a single participant's video/audio tracks with name overlay, mute indicators, active-speaker highlight, volume slider.

Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/LayoutManager.tsx - supports SpeakerView (active speaker focus + sidebar), GridView (equal tiles), SpotlightView (pinned participant). Switchable via prop/state.

Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/Controls.tsx - toolbar with: mic toggle, camera toggle, screen share, raise hand, reactions (emoji picker), push-to-talk (space key), leave button, background blur toggle.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/NetworkQuality.tsx - shows connection quality indicator (signal bars) based on LiveKit connection quality events.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/ChatSidecar.tsx - in-meeting text chat panel using LiveKit DataChannel API for real-time messaging.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/KnockFlow.tsx - UI for locked rooms: participant sends knock request, moderator sees approval dialog.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/Polls.tsx - create/vote/view results for in-meeting polls, data synced via LiveKit data messages.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/BreakoutRoomPanel.tsx - UI for creating/joining breakout rooms, timer, return-to-main button.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/BackgroundBlur.tsx - hook/component that applies MediaPipe selfie segmentation for background blur/virtual background on the local video track.
Create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/index.ts - barrel export for all Media components.
Update packages/shared-ui/src/index.ts (the top-level barrel) to re-export from ./components/Media/index.ts.
Write tests in packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/__tests__/MeetingRoom.test.tsx - mock livekit-client, verify room connection, track publishing, disconnect handling.
Run pnpm typecheck and pnpm test --filter=@quant/shared-ui to verify.
acceptance_criteria: packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/ directory exists with all listed component files
MeetingRoom component connects to LiveKit room, renders participants, handles disconnect
DevicePicker lists and selects media devices
LayoutManager supports speaker, grid, and spotlight views
ChatSidecar sends/receives messages via data channel
Polls component creates and votes on polls
All components exported from packages/shared-ui barrel
findings: livekit-client v2.9.1 installed as dependency. All 11 Media components created (MeetingRoom, DevicePicker, ParticipantTile, LayoutManager, Controls, NetworkQuality, ChatSidecar, KnockFlow, Polls, BreakoutRoomPanel, BackgroundBlur) plus barrel export and main index re-export. 10 MeetingRoom unit tests pass. Full monorepo typecheck (79 tasks) passes. BackgroundBlur defines the hook interface but notes MediaPipe selfie_segmentation as the ideal runtime dep (not installed as it is optional). lint-staged ran prettier on commit.
